narration: You wait until Kelp sets down a crucible before speaking.
response: Kelp glances over with two of their four arms still occupied — one holding tongs, one holding a sandwich. "Yeah?" They take a bite of the sandwich. "If you need something smelted, get in line. If you're buying stock, check the rack by the wall."

"Depends what it is." Kelp taps the side of the nearest crucible with a free hand. "Three crucibles, three temperatures. Most things go in the orange. Weird things go in the blue. Very weird things go in the third one." They gesture at the crucible that isn't quite a color. "Don't stare at that one too long."

"Different dimensional temperatures. Not just hotter — hot in different ways." Kelp pulls something from the orange crucible and drops it in a mold. "Some materials only melt in one. Some melt in all three but come out different each time. You learn which is which."

narration: You look at the rack by the wall.
response: "Slag, mostly. Leftover from the crucibles. Mixed dimensional residue." Kelp shrugs with two shoulders. "Some people find uses for it. Take what you want — it's waste to me."

"Ground to powder, it's a flux. Helps things bond that don't want to bond." Kelp scrapes some residue off a crucible rim. "Three or four dimensions fused together in there. Makes it a good intermediary. I've seen people use it to bridge shimmerite and void glass, which normally won't go near each other."

"Whatever comes through. I can tell what a material needs by the sound it makes." Kelp picks up a lump of something dark and taps it against the crucible rim. It rings high and clear. "Blue crucible. See? It tells you." They toss it in without looking."

narration: You step back from the heat.
response: Kelp grunts and turns back to the crucibles. The sandwich has somehow vanished.
